subject: Cloud Computing
title: Quantum Computing in the Cloud
description: A quick, practical guide to Quantum Computing in the Cloud.

hook.text: IBM, Google, and Amazon offer quantum computers as a cloud service. You can run quantum circuits from your browser.

buildup.text: Quantum computers use qubits that can be 0 and 1 simultaneously, solving certain problems exponentially faster.

discovery.text: AWS Braket, Azure Quantum, and IBM Quantum Network provide access to real quantum hardware through familiar cloud APIs.

twist.text: Current quantum computers are noisy and limited. They can't replace classical computers yet, but hybrids are emerging.

climax.text: Quantum-as-a-service lets researchers experiment without buying million-dollar machines. Cloud democratizes quantum.

punchline.text: Quantum computers in the cloud. Run them from your browser.

quiz.question: How does the cloud make quantum computing accessible?
options: It provides access to real quantum hardware through cloud APIs without buying expensive machines
Cloud providers simulate quantum computing using regular servers
Quantum computers are small enough to fit in every data center
